title: Rozanski (Appellant) v Regional Court 3 Penal Department Poland (Respondent)

metadata.title: Rozanski (Appellant) v Regional Court 3 Penal Department Poland (Respondent)
metadata.summary: Lukaszewski (Appellant) v The District Court in Torun, Poland (Respondent) Pomiechowski (Appellant) v District Court of Legunica 59-220 Poland (Respondent) Rozanski (Appellant) v Regional Court 3 Penal Department Poland (Respondent) R (on the application of Halligen) (Appellant) v Secretary of St...
extracts: Lukaszewski (Appellant) v The District Court in Torun, Poland (Respondent) Pomiechowski (Appellant) v District Court of Legunica 59-220 Poland (Respondent) Rozanski (Appellant) v Regional Court 3 Penal Department Poland (Respondent) R (on the application of Halligen) (Appellant) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent)

1. These appeals raise a number of points, some technical, others fundamental, relating to the requirements of and consequences of non-compliance with the short and inflexible time limits introduced by the Extradition Act 2003. Parts 1 and 2 of that Act deal with extradition to respectively category 1 territories - in practice other member states of the European Union party to the Council Framework Decision of 13 June 2002 (2002/584/JHA) introducing the European Arrest Warrant, to which Part 1 gives effect - and category 2 territories in relation to which a different and more traditional scheme applies.
